summary: Ukraine reported the return of 185 servicemen and one civilian from Russian captivity, while Russia announced the deployment of Iskander‑M missiles with delayed‑detonation sub‑munitions. Multiple UAV incursions were reported across northern, eastern and southern sectors, including attacks from the Black Sea and over the Dnipropetrovsk region. Civilian infrastructure suffered attacks, notably a dairy farm in Kyiv region with four fatalities.
key_points: 185 Ukrainian servicemen and one civilian were exchanged and returned home after captivity, many having fought in Mariupol, Azovstal and multiple front‑line directions (Source: @UkraineNow/81705).
Russia began using Iskander‑M missiles equipped with cassette warheads that detonate 20‑30 minutes after impact, posing delayed‑explosion hazards (Source: @eRadarrua/95485).

Numerous Shahed‑type UAVs were observed over Kyiv, Zhytomyr, Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, with some heading toward southern targets from the Black Sea (Source: @kpszsu/64107, @kpszsu/64115, @eRadarrua/95488).
A Russian attack on a dairy farm in Kyiv region killed four civilians, described as part of a campaign against civilian enterprises (Source: @eRadarrua/95505).

Ukrainian forces reported successful strikes on five vessels in Mariupol and Berdyansk ports, targeting cargo ships and tankers allegedly carrying grain, fuel and military cargo (Source: @UkraineNow/81698).
President Putin claimed Ukraine’s college in Starobelsk contains no military objects, countering Ukrainian statements (Source: @tass_agency/379387).
Ukrainian authorities announced a fundraising drive to repair four damaged intelligence‑unit vehicles, requesting 150,000 UAH (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67498).

sections.executive_summary: 185 Ukrainian servicemen and one civilian were exchanged and returned home after captivity (Source: @UkraineNow/81705). Russia began using Iskander‑M missiles with delayed‑detonation sub‑munitions, increasing hazard zones (Source: @eRadarrua/95485). UAV activity intensified across multiple fronts, with Shahed drones reported over Kyiv, Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv and from the Black Sea toward southern targets (Source: @kpszsu/64107, @kpszsu/64115).

points: The Ukrainian Prisoner‑of‑War exchange returned 185 servicemen and one civilian, many of whom had been held since 2022 after fighting in Mariupol, Azovstal and on the Donetsk, Luhansk, Kharkiv, Kherson, Zaporizhzhia, Sumy, Kyiv and Kursk fronts (Source: @UkraineNow/81705).
A dairy farm in Kyiv region was struck, resulting in four civilian deaths, highlighting attacks on civilian infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/95505).

Ukrainian forces claimed successful drone strikes on five vessels in the ports of Mariupol and Berdyansk, targeting cargo ships and tankers allegedly carrying grain, fuel and military cargo (Source: @UkraineNow/81698).
Ukrainian intelligence units reported damage to four vehicles, prompting a public fundraising appeal for 150,000 UAH to fund repairs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67498).
Air raid alerts were issued over Kyiv on 5 June 2026, indicating ongoing aerial threats (Source: @eRadarrua/95543).
President Putin asserted that the college in Starobelsk contains no military objects, countering Ukrainian claims (Source: @tass_agency/379387).
Russia announced the fielding of Iskander‑M missiles with cassette warheads that detonate 20‑30 minutes after impact, creating delayed‑explosion danger zones (Source: @eRadarrua/95485).
Putin addressed Russian troops, urging them to “work, brothers,” in response to President Zelensky’s letter (Source: @tass_agency/379388).
Russian statements emphasized a policy of “denazification” of Ukraine and claimed to be cooling the Russian economy for its health (Source: @tass_agency/379402, @tass_agency/379395).
Multiple Russian‑controlled UAV groups were reported operating from the Black Sea towards southern Ukrainian areas (Source: @kpszsu/64107).
Shahed‑type UAVs were observed over Dnipropetrovsk region heading toward Apostolove, indicating eastern sector aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/64115).
UAV groups were reported over the northern part of Kharkiv region moving southward, suggesting pressure on eastern front lines (Source: @kpszsu/64115).
A UAV launch was noted from the Black Sea area targeting the southern direction, potentially affecting eastern coastal positions (Source: @kpszsu/64107).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb releases over northern Kharkiv region, indicating intensified air‑ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/64103).
Ukrainian forces reported missile danger in Dnipropetrovsk oblast, reflecting ongoing eastern threats (Source: @kpszsu/64125).
UAVs were sighted moving northward near Buryn in Sumy region, indicating activity along the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/64140).
Shahed UAVs were reported near Chernihiv from the north, suggesting pressure on northern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/64109).
UAVs were observed west of Novhorod‑Siverskyi in Chernihiv oblast moving north, highlighting northern sector incursions (Source: @kpszsu/64115).
New UAVs were detected in northern Chernihiv heading toward Koryukivka, showing continued reconnaissance (Source: @eRadarrua/95530).
A reactive UAV was noted west of Hlukhiv moving toward Konotop, indicating northern operational depth (Source: @kpszsu/64102).
UAV groups launched from the Black Sea were reported heading toward southern Ukrainian targets (Source: @kpszsu/64107).
An attack by a UAV group from the Black Sea was noted near Vylkove, indicating southern maritime pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/95535).
A missile strike was reported in Mykolaiv oblast involving a UAV group directed toward Odesa direction (Source: @eRadarrua/95550).
Thirteen Shahed drones were observed over Zhytomyr moving toward Rivne/Korosten, reflecting southern‑to‑northwest trajectories (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67492).
Four civilian deaths occurred at a dairy farm in Kyiv region, a southern‑ward target of Russian strikes (Source: @eRadarrua/95505).
Air raid alerts were issued over Kyiv on 5 June 2026, confirming ongoing aerial threats (Source: @eRadarrua/95543).
Ukrainian forces reported successful drone strikes on five vessels in Mariupol and Berdyansk ports, indicating effective air‑to‑sea operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81698).
Russian tactical aviation conducted guided bomb releases over northern Kharkiv, showing continued use of air‑delivered munitions (Source: @kpszsu/64103).
Iskander‑M missiles with delayed‑detonation sub‑munitions were deployed, adding a new dimension to the missile threat (Source: @eRadarrua/95485).

Multiple Shahed UAV launches were reported across Kyiv, Zhytomyr, Chernihiv, Sumy and Dnipropetrovsk regions, indicating widespread aerial activity (Source: @kpszsu/64107, @kpszsu/64115, @eRadarrua/95488).

key_developments: 185 Ukrainian servicemen and one civilian were exchanged and returned home after captivity, many having fought in Mariupol, Azovstal and on multiple front‑line directions (Source: @UkraineNow/81705).
Russia began employing Iskander‑M missiles with cassette warheads that detonate 20‑30 minutes after impact, creating delayed‑explosion hazards (Source: @eRadarrua/95485).

Shahed‑type UAVs were observed over Kyiv, Zhytomyr, Chernihiv, Sumy, Kharkiv and Dnipropetrovsk regions, with some groups launching from the Black Sea toward southern targets (Source: @kpszsu/64107, @kpszsu/64115).
A Russian strike on a dairy farm in Kyiv region killed four civilians, illustrating attacks on civilian infrastructure (Source: @eRadarrua/95505).
Ukrainian forces reported drone strikes on five vessels in Mariupol and Berdyansk ports, targeting cargo ships and tankers carrying grain, fuel and military cargo (Source: @UkraineNow/81698).
President Putin claimed the college in Starobelsk contains no military objects, countering Ukrainian assertions (Source: @tass_agency/379387).
Ukrainian intelligence units reported damage to four vehicles, prompting a public fundraising appeal for 150,000 UAH for repairs (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67498).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian deployment of Iskander‑M missiles with delayed‑detonation sub‑munitions raises the risk of secondary explosions and complicates civilian evacuation. The proliferation of Shahed UAVs across northern, eastern and southern sectors indicates a sustained aerial reconnaissance and strike capability, pressuring Ukrainian air defenses. Ukrainian counter‑drone and maritime drone operations show adaptability but face increasing Russian air‑to‑ground and missile threats.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have intensified air‑defence alerts, especially over Kyiv, and coordinated drone interceptions in multiple oblasts. Local administrations in Kyiv region are addressing civilian casualties from the dairy farm attack, while maritime units in Mariupol and Berdyansk have reported successful interdiction of hostile vessels. Intelligence units are seeking rapid repairs of damaged vehicles to maintain operational mobility.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths reveals coordinated launches from the Black Sea and inland bases, suggesting a layered approach to aerial pressure. The use of Iskander‑M missiles with time‑delayed sub‑munitions may be intended to hinder rescue and de‑mining efforts. Continued claims by Russian leadership about non‑military status of certain facilities aim to shape information narratives, requiring verification.
